summary: Matched two small `melee/gr/grcorneria.c` functions, raising the file report to 1.81% matched for a 25.39 KB file. The new C implements `grCorneria_801E0C3C` and `grCorneria_801E2EEC`, adds the minimal header/type support needed for those functions, and marks five related scratch entries as `OK`. The diff also refreshes the large `asm/melee/gr/grcorneria.s` reference formatting with `.fn/.endfn`, `.obj/.endobj`, `.file`, `.text`, and normalized immediates/data syntax.

key_files.reason: Added includes, a local `clearX10_b2` inline helper, and the matched C bodies for `grCorneria_801E0C3C` and `grCorneria_801E2EEC`.
Added `struct grCorneria_GroundVars` with padding to `gp+C4` and `HSD_JObj* x68` at `gp+12C`, then added it to the `Ground::gv` union as `corneria`.
Added `JOBJ_UNK_B27 (1 << 27)`, used as a material/JObj flag in the matched stage setup code.
Updated five scratch entries from partial percentages to `OK`, including `func_801DCCFC`, `grCorneria_801DD508`, `lbl_801E0C3C`, and `lbl_801E2EEC`.
Large assembly reference refresh/reformat across the Corneria stage file; evidence shows `.fn/.endfn` wrapping and `.obj/.endobj` data objects.

what_changed: Added `grCorneria_801E0C3C(HSD_GObj* gobj)`, which obtains `Ground*` and `HSD_JObj*`, runs `grAnime_801C8138(gobj, gp->map_id, 0)`, clears `GET_GROUND(gobj)->x10_flags.b2`, wires six `Ground_801C3FA4` results into `Ground_801C4E70`, applies `grMaterial_801C8A04(jobj, JOBJ_UNK_B27)` and `grMaterial_801C8858(jobj, JOBJ_ROOT_XLU)`, and sets `gp->x11_flags.b012 = 2`.

Added `grCorneria_801E2EEC(Vec3* v, u32 arg1, HSD_JObj* jobj)`, which calls `lb_8000B1CC` to get a JObj position, looks up ground object id `3` with `Ground_801C2BA4(3)`, checks `temp_r3_2->gv.corneria.x68 == jobj`, performs Corneria-specific scaled float tests using `106.0f`, `107.0f`, and repeated `Ground_801C0498()` calls, then returns a boolean based on x/y comparisons.
Introduced `struct grCorneria_GroundVars` with `u8 _0[0x68]` padding and `HSD_JObj* x68`, documenting offsets as `gp+C4` and `gp+12C`.
Extended the `Ground` ground-vars union with `struct grCorneria_GroundVars corneria`.
Added the `grMaterial_801C8A04` prototype and the `JOBJ_UNK_B27` bit flag required by the new C code.
Changed scratches for five existing addresses from partial match percentages such as `97.54%` and `99.73%` to `OK`.
Reformatted `asm/melee/gr/grcorneria.s` from older `.global`/label and `.section .text` style toward `.file "grcorneria.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn/.endfn`, and object-wrapped data labels.
smart_moves: Kept the new C small and targeted: only two 100% functions were added to a large 25.39 KB stage file, reducing review and matching risk.
Added only the header surface needed by the matched functions instead of broad type churn: one material prototype, one JObj flag, and one Corneria-specific ground-vars struct.

Used a stage-specific `Ground::gv.corneria` union member with explicit padding/offset comments, letting the C express `temp_r3_2->gv.corneria.x68` without pretending the rest of the Corneria ground variables are understood.
Factored the bitfield clear into `static inline void clearX10_b2(HSD_GObj* gobj)`, making the intent readable while still preserving the bitfield operation shape needed by the match.

Preserved suspicious-looking float/codegen details in `grCorneria_801E2EEC`, including repeated `Ground_801C0498()` calls and separate temporaries, which is often necessary for exact PowerPC scheduling/register allocation.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching one function may require small but precise additions across `types.h`, stage headers, and baselib flags; do not force anonymous offsets in the C when a narrowly scoped union member can document the access.
When only one field of a stage-specific ground-vars payload is known, padding plus an offset-commented field is acceptable evidence-grounded structure work.
Bitfield writes such as `x10_flags.b2 = false` and `x11_flags.b012 = 2` can be made readable and still match when the underlying `Ground` flags are already modeled.
Material/JObj flag discoveries should be promoted to shared headers only at the bit actually used; here `JOBJ_UNK_B27` was enough to support `grMaterial_801C8A04` usage.
Scratch entries should be updated to `OK` after functions reach 100%, even when older scratch names are generic labels like `lbl_801E0C3C` or `func_801DCCFC`.
naming_conventions: Stage functions retain address-based names in the `grCorneria_801E0C3C` / `grCorneria_801E2EEC` style until semantic names are known.
Corneria globals and labels use the existing `grCn_` prefix in assembly/data references.
Stage-specific ground variable structs follow the pattern `struct grCorneria_GroundVars` and are exposed in the `Ground::gv` union as lowercase `corneria`.
Unknown JObj flags continue the `JOBJ_UNK_BNN` convention, here adding `JOBJ_UNK_B27`.
Temporary local names in the matched function remain compiler/decompiler-style (`temp_r3`, `temp_r3_2`, `temp_f31`, `temp_f31_2`) rather than invented semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched `grCorneria_801E0C3C` as a compact setup function by using the existing ground/JObj accessor macros and direct flag bitfields.
Matched `grCorneria_801E2EEC` with explicit stack local `Vec3 sp14`, separate float temporaries, literal `106.0f`/`107.0f`, and boolean `return 0`/`return 1` control flow.
Kept the `arg1` parameter in `grCorneria_801E2EEC` even though the shown C body does not use it, preserving the function signature implied by call sites/ABI.
Used `Ground_801C2BA4(3)` and a NULL-guarded `user_data` cast before accessing `gv.corneria.x68`, matching the assembly's defensive pointer checks.

The assembly reference was normalized with `.fn/.endfn` function boundaries and `.obj/.endobj` data objects, improving symbol searchability and future split/decomp work, although the PR evidence does not explain whether this was manual or generated.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there is no review discussion, any motivation beyond the title/report/diff is weak evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or expand `struct grCorneria_GroundVars` beyond the padded `x68` field.
Find later uses of `JOBJ_UNK_B27` to determine its semantic name.
Find later declarations or matches around `grMaterial_801C8A04` to improve its prototype/name.
Search subsequent `grcorneria` PRs for functions near `801E0C3C` and `801E2EEC` to see whether this setup/collision logic gets semantic names.
Check whether the `.fn/.endfn` and `.obj/.endobj` assembly formatting refresh was part of a broader assembly tooling migration.
